commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh When you build a new house, then you shall make a parapet for your roof, that you bring not the guilt of blood upon your house, if any man fall from there. This precaution was necessary, because all their houses had flat tops; and it was usual to walk and to converse together upon them. (Challoner) King Ochozias had the misfortune to fall from the top of his house, (4 Kings i. 2,) and David saw Bethsabee when he was walking on the roof of his palace, 2 Kings xi. Saul slept at the top of Samuel's house, 1 Kings ix. See Josue ii. , and Matthew x.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Battlement: This precaution was necessary, because all their houses had flat tops, and it was usual to walk and to converse together upon them.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
